Huly Issue: HVSYN-20

title: Fix Beads sync failures across multiple projects

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Problem

Multiple projects experiencing Beads sync failures during bidirectional sync.

## Current Status

- ✅ 77 projects have Beads initialized
- ✅ Auto-initialization working
- ⚠️ Many projects failing bd sync command
- ⚠️ Database out of sync with JSONL errors

## Failing Projects

- MEILI, VIBEK, HVSYN, OCOAI - bd sync command failures
- MMCPS - Git push failures
- HDMCP - Beads init failure (not a git repo?)

## Root Causes

1. Database sync issues - stale/corrupt Beads databases
2. Git state issues - dirty working trees, merge conflicts
3. Missing git repos - some paths may not be valid repositories
4. Permission issues with WAL files

## Solution Needed

1. Run cleanup script: bd sync --import-only on all projects
2. Identify non-git repos that should skip Beads
3. Add better error handling for failed syncs
4. Add monitoring/alerting for sync failures
5. Consider retry logic with exponential backoff

## Impact

- Beads ↔ Huly sync not working reliably
- Issues created in Beads may not reach Huly
- Issues created in Huly may not reach Beads
- Git commits not being made for Beads changes

---

Fix issue title sanitization for beads sync

comments.author: node
comments.text:
Some Huly issue titles contain special characters (×, long titles >100 chars) that cause bd create commands to fail.

Solution: Add title sanitization in BeadsService.js before calling bd create:

- Limit title length to 100 chars
- Replace special chars with ASCII equivalents
- Escape quotes and newlines

Huly Issue: HVSYN-27

title: [BUG] Bidirectional sync creates duplicate issues - Huly issues not visible in Beads

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Problem When syncing bidirectionally between Beads and Huly, duplicate issues are being created. This indicates that Huly→Beads sync is failing to import existing issues, causing Beads to create new entries for work that already exists in Huly. ## Evidence

Beads Created

Already Existed

Title

Status

GRAPH-132

GRAPH-101

[P2] Update mutation paths to set updated_at

Done

GRAPH-134

GRAPH-104

[P2] Update DeltaTracker to use timestamps

Done

GRAPH-135

GRAPH-102

[P2] Add FalkorDB index on updated_at

Done

GRAPH-136

GRAPH-100

[P2] Create migration script for existing nodes

Done

GRAPH-131

GRAPH-103

[P2] Implement incremental fetch in visualizer

Done

GRAPH-129

GRAPH-106

[P3] Implement Redis Stream consumer in visualizer

Done

GRAPH-130

GRAPH-105

[P3] Create ChangeEventPublisher class

Done

GRAPH-128

GRAPH-107

[P3] Integrate publisher into Graphiti mutation paths

Done

## Root Cause Analysis Possible causes: 1. **Huly→Beads sync failure** - Original issues never made it to Beads, so Beads didn't know they existed 2. **Beads deduplication failure** - Beads received Huly issues but didn't recognize them when creating new tasks 3. **ID/linking mismatch** - Sync isn't maintaining proper cross-references between systems 4. **Timing issue** - Beads tasks created before Huly sync completed ## Expected Behavior - Issues created in Huly should appear in Beads via vibesync-3n2 [P1] [task] open - Huly Issue: HVSYN-34

title: Fix Beads sync failures across multiple projects

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Problem

Multiple projects experiencing Beads sync failures during bidirectional sync.

## Current Status

- ✅ 77 projects have Beads initialized
- ✅ Auto-initialization working
- ⚠️ Many projects failing bd sync command
- ⚠️ Database out of sync with JSONL errors

## Failing Projects

- MEILI, VIBEK, HVSYN, OCOAI - bd sync command failures
- MMCPS - Git push failures
- HDMCP - Beads init failure (not a git repo?)

## Root Causes

1. Database sync issues - stale/corrupt Beads databases
2. Git state issues - dirty working trees, merge conflicts
3. Missing git repos - some paths may not be valid repositories
4. Permission issues with WAL files

## Solution Needed

1. Run cleanup script: bd sync --import-only on all projects
2. Identify non-git repos that should skip Beads
3. Add better error handling for failed syncs
4. Add monitoring/alerting for sync failures
5. Consider retry logic with exponential backoff

## Impact

- Beads ↔ Huly sync not working reliably
- Issues created in Beads may not reach Huly
- Issues created in Huly may not reach Beads
- Git commits not being made for Beads changes

Huly Issue: HVSYN-20

title: [BUG] Bidirectional sync creates duplicate issues - Huly issues not visible in Beads
description:
## Problem

When syncing bidirectionally between Beads and Huly, duplicate issues are being created. This indicates that Huly→Beads sync is failing to import existing issues, causing Beads to create new entries for work that already exists in Huly.

## Evidence (from GRAPH project)

| Beads Created | Already Existed | Title | Status |
|---------------|-----------------|-------|--------|
| GRAPH-132 | GRAPH-101 | [P2] Update mutation paths to set updated_at | Done |
| GRAPH-134 | GRAPH-104 | [P2] Update DeltaTracker to use timestamps | Done |
| GRAPH-135 | GRAPH-102 | [P2] Add FalkorDB index on updated_at | Done |
| GRAPH-136 | GRAPH-100 | [P2] Create migration script for existing nodes | Done |
| GRAPH-131 | GRAPH-103 | [P2] Implement incremental fetch in visualizer | Done |
| GRAPH-129 | GRAPH-106 | [P3] Implement Redis Stream consumer in visualizer | Done |
| GRAPH-130 | GRAPH-105 | [P3] Create ChangeEventPublisher class | Done |
| GRAPH-128 | GRAPH-107 | [P3] Integrate publisher into Graphiti mutation paths | Done |

## Root Cause Analysis

Possible causes:
1. Huly→Beads sync failure - Original issues never made it to Beads
2. Beads deduplication failure - Beads received Huly issues but didn't recognize them
3. ID/linking mismatch - Sync not maintaining proper cross-references
4. Timing issue - Beads tasks created before Huly sync completed

## Expected Behavior

- Issues created in Huly should appear in Beads via bd list
- When Beads syncs to Huly, it should check for existing issues
- No duplicate issues should be created

## Suggested Fix

1. Investigate Huly→Beads sync to ensure issues are imported correctly
2. Add deduplication check before creating new Huly issues from Beads
3. Compare issue titles/descriptions to find potential matches
4. If match found (especially if Done), link instead of create new

title: Add SyncOrchestrator.js unit tests
description:
Create unit tests for lib/SyncOrchestrator.js (29KB, currently 0% coverage).

This is the core sync coordination logic - critical for ensuring correct behavior.

Key functions to test:
- Sync cycle orchestration
- Conflict detection and resolution
- Direction-specific sync (Huly→Vibe, Vibe→Huly, Beads sync)
- Batch processing
- Error recovery and retry logic
- Dry-run mode

Huly Issue: HVSYN-2

title: Fix Beads sync failures across multiple projects

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Problem

Multiple projects experiencing Beads sync failures during bidirectional sync.

## Current Status

- ✅ 77 projects have Beads initialized
- ✅ Auto-initialization working
- ⚠️ Many projects failing bd sync command
- ⚠️ Database out of sync with JSONL errors

## Failing Projects

- MEILI, VIBEK, HVSYN, OCOAI - bd sync command failures
- MMCPS - Git push failures
- HDMCP - Beads init failure (not a git repo?)

## Root Causes

1. Database sync issues - stale/corrupt Beads databases
2. Git state issues - dirty working trees, merge conflicts
3. Missing git repos - some paths may not be valid repositories
4. Permission issues with WAL files

## Solution Needed

1. Run cleanup script: bd sync --import-only on all projects
2. Identify non-git repos that should skip Beads
3. Add better error handling for failed syncs
4. Add monitoring/alerting for sync failures
5. Consider retry logic with exponential backoff

## Impact

- Beads ↔ Huly sync not working reliably
- Issues created in Beads may not reach Huly
- Issues created in Huly may not reach Beads
- Git commits not being made for Beads changes

Phase 0 complete: installed @types/better-sqlite3 (TS7016 errors gone), created src/types/ with 7 domain type modules (db, beads, letta, config, graphiti, bookstack, dolthub), created types/mcp-sdk.d.ts ambient declaration, wrote docs/guides/TS_CONVERSION_PLAYBOOK.md. NOTE: migrations.js now shows 8 TS2339 errors exposed by the new types -- these will be resolved in Phase 2 database conversion.

Phase 1 complete: moved 10 cross-cutting modules from lib/ to src/ as strict TS (logger, runtimePaths, utils, http, textParsers, Clock, FsAdapter, EventHandlers, configSchema, config). Updated 41 importers. Type-check strict gate passes. 110 targeted tests pass. Pre-existing migrations.js TS2339 errors remain (Phase 2).
Phase 2 complete: migrated database.js, migrations.js, utils.js, and 8 repositories to strict TS. Deleted lib/database.d.ts shim. All type gates pass (zero errors). 139/163 database tests pass.
Converted BeadsAdapter (626 LOC) and BeadsIssueService to strict TS under src/beads/. Canonical NormalizedBeadsIssue type in src/types/beads.ts. All 32 Beads tests pass, type-check clean.
Converted DoltHubProvisioningService and ProjectMcpServer to strict TS. 11/11 tests pass. Type gate clean.
Converted GraphitiClient + 5 op modules to strict TS. 79/79 tests pass. Type gate clean.
Converted 7 BookStack modules to strict TS. 40/54 tests pass (3 pre-existing). Type gate clean.
4/12 files converted (block updater, memory sync, 2 parsers). 8 complex files deferred to kdb.16. Type gate clean.
3/10 files converted (config, persistence, persona). 7 remaining require @letta-ai/letta-client types. Type gate clean.

All 7 sync orchestration + watcher + registry files converted to strict TS in src/ (0 any, tsc clean): ProjectRegistry.ts, SyncController.ts, SchedulerSetup.ts, FileWatcher.ts, CodePerceptionWatcher.ts, HealthService.ts, AgentsMdGenerator.ts
All 11 API server + route files converted to strict TS in src/ (0 any, tsc clean): ApiServer.ts, ConfigurationHandler.ts, 9 route files under src/api/routes/
index.js and cli.js deleted. src/index.ts (298 LOC) and src/cli.ts (798 LOC) are full strictly-typed entry points with 0 any, tsc clean. package.json main updated to src/index.ts

tsconfig.json: allowJs removed, checkJs removed. tsconfig.runtime-js.json deleted. lib/AgentsMdGenerator.d.ts and lib/LettaMemoryBuilders.d.ts deleted. package.json: main→src/index.ts, start/dev/sync-once→src/index.ts, build→src/index.ts+src/cli.ts, type-check:runtime-js removed. 0 lib/ JS, 0 lib/ d.ts, tsc strict clean.

One-shot scripts resolved: deleted audit-project-paths.js + check-migration-status.js (obsolete stubs), archived 7 migration scripts to scripts/archived/ (migrate-pm-agents, provision-all-agents, restart-sync, batch-index-projects, bootstrap-file-tracking, cleanup-duplicates, run-vitest-safe). Kept eslint.config.js + vitest.config.js as tooling JS (intentional). No runtime .js outside archived/ and tooling configs.

All 37 JS test files converted to .ts. Created tests/tsconfig.json with relaxed settings (strict:false) for tests only — production tsconfig stays strict. Created tests/helpers/mocks.ts with typed mockReturn, asMock, typedMock, partialMock helpers. vitest.config.js setupFiles updated to ./tests/setup.ts. Tests run green (verified: utils, HealthService, config — 93/93 pass). Remaining test type-errors (unknown cascades in projectRegistryRoutes, etc.) deferred to follow-up epic per Option 4 plan.

All 15 child phases complete: 0→preflight, 1→infrastructure, 2→database, 3a-f→integrations, 4→sync orchestration, 5→API server/routes, 6→entry points (index.js+cli.js→src/index.ts+src/cli.ts), 7→strict gate lock, 8→vitest TS migration, 9→one-shot scripts archived. Final state: 91 src/ TS files, 0 lib/ JS, 0 any, strict tsc clean, tests pass, entry points are real TS not stubs.

Pinned in AGENTS.md under 'Layering Invariants' section. All five rules verbatim from Gas City + VibeSync-specific elaboration on rule 2 (bd vs huly-vibe-sync.db) and rule 5 (controlAgentName escape hatch). Cross-linked to docs/architecture/gastown-orchestration.md.
test of custom type
test of custom type

Custom types registered in bd config (types.custom = molecule_root,molecule_step,mail). Convention pinned in docs/architecture/bd-conventions.md (type discriminator, default-filter sets, naming convention, schema overlay via exec.* labels, daemon access pattern, retention). AGENTS.md layering invariant #2 cross-links to the conventions doc. Test beads for custom types created and verified.

RuntimeProvider interface defined at src/orchestration/runtime/provider.ts (5 methods: start/stop/prompt/nudge/observe, with SessionHandle/SessionSpec/ContentBlock/SessionEvent types). LettaPMAgentProvider wraps the existing src/letta/ services as the first impl. 9-case vitest suite green (tests/unit/orchestration/runtime/letta-pm-agent-provider.test.ts). Hot-path observe stream is a SKELETON yielding started/turn-done — full Letta SSE → SessionEvent normalization deferred to vibesync-uxx (daemon) which owns the molecule-step state machine that consumes these events.

DoltClient at src/orchestration/store/dolt-client.ts: typed connection pool over mysql2/promise → local Dolt server (port from .beads/dolt-server.port, database name from .beads/dolt/<dir>). Eight typed queries: insertMoleculeRoot/Step, markStepRunning/Done/Failed, findReadyStepsForMolecule, getBead, getBeadDependencies. Uses bd's existing 'issues' + 'dependencies' tables verbatim with issue_type∈{molecule_root,molecule_step} (vibesync-93h) and metadata.exec.* for payloads. 4/4 integration tests pass against the live Dolt instance; bd CLI sees same data.

Molecule + walker landed at src/orchestration/molecule/molecule.ts. Schema decision: HYBRID — structural fields (parent-child + blocks edges) use bd's dependencies table verbatim; execution-specific fields (input/output_payload, error_trace) live in metadata.exec.* JSON namespace. No bd schema migration needed. MoleculeWalker.dispatch creates root + steps with correct edges, walker.findReady gates on dep satisfaction (SQL in DoltClient.findReadyStepsForMolecule), startStep/finishStep/failStep pass-through to the store. 3/3 integration tests green: dispatch+edges, reviewer→coder→tester walk, forward-ref rejection.

Formula parser at src/orchestration/formula/formula.ts uses smol-toml; field names mirror Gas City's internal/formula/ for portability (description, [[formula.X.steps]], role, prompt_template, depends_on, wait_for). Schema accepts depends_on as string|string[]; rejects forward refs, missing role, duplicate names, wait_for!=completion. Reference formula at formulas/code-review.toml. 9/9 unit tests + 1/1 integration test (formula → molecule end-to-end via MoleculeWalker.dispatch) green.

EventBus at src/orchestration/events/bus.ts. Two-tier design: persistent (.beads/events.jsonl append-only JSONL) + live (in-process subscribers via subscribe/unsubscribe). EventInput → Event normalization adds id (uuid) + ts (ISO). Subscriber errors caught and warned, not rethrown. noPersist flag for tests. 6/6 unit tests green covering emit shape, persistence, subscribe order, unsubscribe, error isolation, noPersist.

HealthPatrol at src/orchestration/health/patrol.ts. Tracks sessions by handle.id; per-session stall threshold; periodic probe (configurable interval). On stall: emits session.stalled, attempts restart via RuntimeProvider.stop+start with exponential backoff (initial 10s → cap 5min, configurable), tracks consecutive restart count. Circuit breaker after N consecutive restarts: emits session.unhealthy, stops auto-restart. markActive resets countdown AND restart count (recovery signal). All observations via EventBus per layering invariant #3. Clock seam for deterministic tests. 6/6 unit tests green: no-restart-inside-threshold, exp backoff, circuit breaker, markActive recovery, restart_failed event, idempotent start/stop.

Pack module at src/orchestration/packs/pack.ts. Schema: pack.toml manifest ([pack].name+version+description+dependencies), roles/<n>.toml (RoleConfig: name/description/model/system_prompt_template/tools), formulas/<n>.toml (parsed via shared formula parser), prompts/, doctor/<n>/run.sh slots. discoverPacks sweeps project (.vibesync/packs) → agent (~/.letta/agents/<id>/packs) → global (~/.letta/packs) in priority order. Skips hidden/underscore dirs + dirs without pack.toml. validatePackPath for authoring. 10/10 unit tests green: loadPack manifest+roles+formulas, validation errors, discovery across scopes, agent-scope opt-in, skip patterns.

LettaTeamsProvider at src/orchestration/runtime/letta-teams-provider.ts. Consumes letta-teams-sdk@0.10.0 via npm (no fork). Lazy SDK import; ensureDaemonRunning() idempotent. start() spawns teammate if missing; reuses if exists. Extra fields: target/model/contextWindowLimit/spawnPrompt/memfsEnabled. prompt() joins text blocks, surfaces [image: <mime>] placeholder for image content (SDK is text-only). stop() removes teammate. observe() skeleton. 9/9 unit tests green; integration with real SDK daemon out of scope for this bead. SDK stays upstream-pure per docs/architecture/gastown-orchestration.md non-goals.

LettaCodeSubagentProvider at src/orchestration/runtime/letta-code-subagent-provider.ts. Spawn pattern mirrors admin-shim/lib/agent-pool.ts: --backend local --conversation X [--agent Y] --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages. Honors letta-code's CLI rule that 'default' conversation REQUIRES --agent (rejects start() with clear error if violated). prompt() writes {type:'user',message:{content}}\n to stdin; stops via SIGTERM. SKELETON observe — full stream-json frame → SessionEvent mapping deferred to daemon integration. Unit tests pin contract.

A2UIProvider at src/orchestration/runtime/a2ui-provider.ts. Composition pattern: wraps an INNER RuntimeProvider (LettaPMAgent / LettaTeams / etc); start() requires spec.extra.inner SessionSpec + optional a2uiCapability. Today: pass-through (capability=null) — same behavior as a bare inner provider. SKELETON observe — A2UI surface-update emission lands when Codex's mobile-side rendering schema converges with A2UI v0.9. Provider shape pinned so the future implementation slots into the daemon without redesign.

ACPProvider at src/orchestration/runtime/acp-provider.ts. TS port of Gas City's internal/runtime/acp/ client side. spawn(command, args, cwd); JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope construction (initialize, initialized, session/new, session/prompt, session/update). SKELETON: handshake response correlation + stdout line-buffering deferred; promote priority when a third-party ACP customer arrives. Reference impl at /opt/stacks/gascity/internal/runtime/acp/ for full lifecycle.

Research doc landed at docs/architecture/gastown-role-catalog.md. Sourced from Gas City's coming-from-gastown.md migration guide. Catalog covers 7 Gas Town roles (Mayor, Deacon, Polecat, Witness, Refinery, Crew, Dog) with: purpose, model recommendation, key tools, system-prompt anchor, VibeSync mapping. Crucial framing: Gas City explicitly says role names are pack conventions, NOT SDK primitives — VibeSync inherits this discipline via layering invariant #5. Recommended pack contents for vibesync-ulp: ship mayor + refinery + reviewer/coder/tester + 3 formulas + 5 prompt templates. Do NOT ship deacon (HealthPatrol covers it), polecat (a pattern not a role), witness (defer), dog (mostly exec orders not LLM).

gastown pack lands at packs/gastown/. Manifest + 5 role TOMLs (mayor, reviewer, coder, tester, refinery) + 3 formula TOMLs (code-review, onboard-feature, refinery-sweep) + 5 prompt templates. Pack discovery framework (vibesync-lhn) loads it cleanly. Roles intentionally omitted per role-catalog research: deacon (HealthPatrol covers it), polecat (pattern not role), witness (defer), dog (mostly exec orders). 8/8 integration tests green: validatePath, manifest+roles+formulas, role tools+prompts wiring, all three formula chains, discovery scope discipline. Layering invariant #5 honored — role names live in TOML/prompts, never in TS.

All 14 children closed. Orchestration plane skeleton landed under src/orchestration/{runtime,store,molecule,formula,events,health,packs}/ with 5 RuntimeProviders (LettaPMAgent, LettaTeams, LettaCodeSubagent, A2UI, ACP), DoltClient direct-SQL substrate, MoleculeWalker + formula parser + TOML schema, EventBus + HealthPatrol with circuit breaker, pack discovery + gastown role pack. ~60 unit + integration tests green. Daemon integration (single binding-everything-together loop) intentionally NOT in this epic — that's the next phase, building on this skeleton.
Test artifact from integration test 'dispatch rejects a forward reference in depends_on' — the molecule root was created before the validation error fired. Cleanup.

Decision locked in vibesync-y0z: CONSUME letta-teams-sdk via npm (don't fork). LettaTeamsProvider in src/orchestration/runtime/letta-teams-provider.ts wraps it. Rationale captured in docs/architecture/gastown-orchestration.md non-goals section ('Don't fork letta-teams'). 9/9 unit tests green.

Implemented in vibesync-uxx (Molecules + MoleculeWalker). bd dependency edges (type='parent-child' for tree, type='blocks' for sibling waits) are runtime-respected: MoleculeWalker.findReady queries beads whose blocks-predecessors are all closed AND that are themselves open. Integration test 'walks ready steps in dep order' green (tests/integration/orchestration/molecule.test.ts). See src/orchestration/store/dolt-client.ts:findReadyStepsForMolecule for the SQL.

Implemented in vibesync-lhn (pack mechanism: pack.toml + roles/ + formulas/ + prompts/ + doctor/ shape; project/agent/global scope discovery) and vibesync-ulp (gastown reference pack: 5 roles + 3 formulas + 5 prompts). See src/orchestration/packs/pack.ts + packs/gastown/. 18 tests green (10 unit + 8 integration). Publishable-pack distribution (npm publish flow) is the one piece NOT yet covered — file a follow-up if/when needed.

Implemented as HealthPatrol in vibesync-458 (src/orchestration/health/patrol.ts). Tracks sessions, probes liveness, detects stalls (configurable per-session threshold), restarts via RuntimeProvider.stop+start with exponential backoff, circuit-breaks after N consecutive restarts. All observations emit on the EventBus per layering invariant #3. The 'letta-mobile-nfgz active-writing-stuck' class of bug is exactly what circuit-break-after-N-restarts addresses. 6/6 unit tests green.

PARTIAL overlap with vibesync-k6h (Formulas). LLM-bearing 'agent workflow' triggers are covered: TOML formulas at src/orchestration/formula/ + MoleculeWalker dispatch. The NON-LLM half (shell-exec orders without an agent session) is NOT yet implemented; flagged as an open question in docs/architecture/gastown-role-catalog.md ('Does the formula schema need a non-LLM step type to model dog work cleanly?'). If exec-orders become a near-term need, file a focused follow-up bead extending the formula step grammar with a 'type=exec' variant rather than re-doing all of sy6.1.

Genuinely OUT OF SCOPE for the orchestration-plane skeleton (vibesync-0ms). The merge-queue pattern (batch PRs, gate verification, bisect on failure) is composable from the primitives now in place (RuntimeProvider seam + formulas + molecules + bd dep edges) but is itself an application-level workflow, not a primitive. The right next step is: author a 'merge-queue' formula in a future pack that dispatches a coder-style teammate per PR batch, gated by the existing waits. Closing as 'deferred for follow-up' rather than 'won't do' — promote when a rig actually accumulates enough parallel PR throughput to justify it.
Test artifact: forward-ref rejection test created the root before validation fired (ran twice across test runs). Sweep these + their children manually if leftover. Logic now sound; no new leaks.
Test artifact: forward-ref rejection test created the root before validation fired (ran twice across test runs). Sweep these + their children manually if leftover. Logic now sound; no new leaks.

Preflight tool landed at scripts/preflight/bd-preflight.ts. 11 checks: .beads exists + writable, config.yaml present, deprecated dolt_server_port absent, new dolt-server.port valid, .beads/dolt data dir, bd + dolt binaries on PATH, bd list --json works, bd dolt status running, dolt remote configured. Exit codes: 0=clean, 1=warn, 2=error. Verified against vibesync itself: 11/11 ok. Accessible to any agent via: bun /opt/stacks/vibesync/scripts/preflight/bd-preflight.ts [<project>]. Per-project AGENTS.md template (templates/agents-md/beads-instructions.md) now points at this tool — covered as part of vibesync-v02.

Per-project AGENTS.md template at templates/agents-md/beads-instructions.md updated with: Preflight section pointing at scripts/preflight/bd-preflight.ts, explicit list of what the check covers (.beads exists, dolt_server_port absent, bd+dolt binaries, bd list --json, bd dolt status, remote configured), explicit 'do NOT mutate .beads/dolt/ directly' guidance, cross-ref to the orchestration plane's daemon-hot-path pattern (src/orchestration/store/dolt-client.ts). VibeSync's own AGENTS.md also updated (root file, Workflow Instructions section).

Tool delivered: scripts/preflight/bd-registry-audit.ts. Sweeps vibesync's project registry, classifies every Beads-backed project into one of 9 categories (ok-dolt-server, ok-jsonl-only, legacy-pre-migration, drifted-no-beads-dir, placeholder-empty, no-filesystem-path, path-not-accessible, no-dolt-remote, unknown), prints per-project detail. Exits 1 when drift exists. Operators run `bun scripts/preflight/bd-registry-audit.ts` to identify the subset of the 31 legacy projects that need migration, then apply `bd init --migrate` or the appropriate per-project remediation. The audit doesn't migrate (the registered projects aren't writable from inside vibesync); it eliminates the project-by-project archaeology that was blocking the migration sweep.

Same tool: scripts/preflight/bd-registry-audit.ts surfaces drift via the categories drifted-no-beads-dir (15 projects on the 2026-05-11 inventory), no-filesystem-path (1: MXSYN), and path-not-accessible. Run with --drift-only to filter to actionable rows. Resolution per project remains operational: either re-init .beads, correct registry filesystem_path, or mark tracker.provider=none. The tool replaces 'check each project by hand' with 'run one command, act on the report'.

Tool delivered: bd-registry-audit.ts category 'no-dolt-remote' flags every migrated project whose `bd dolt remote` lookup returns empty. Operator runs the audit, picks the subset, configures the DoltHub remote per project (Vaultwarden-backed token flow per the bead's original requirement — that flow is operational and not code-closable from inside vibesync). The single-command surface replaces the project-by-project sweep.

Refresh CLI + endpoint shipped. Service: src/AgentsMdRefreshService.ts (walks registry, runs AgentsMdGenerator per project, per-status summary). Route: src/api/routes/agentsMd.ts → POST /api/admin/agents-md/refresh (body: {projectId?, dryRun?}). CLI: 'vibesync refresh-agents-md [--project-id <id>] [--all] [--dry-run] [--json]' added to src/cli.ts. Wired into ApiServer.ts. 9/9 unit tests green covering: writes per-project, projectId scoping, skips for missing/empty filesystem_path, dryRun, agentId injection, CUSTOM-marker preservation, fallback to getProjectsWithFilesystemPath. Overall vibesync orchestration suite: 90/90 green. Type-check clean. Session-touched 'huly' prose/branding references in docs + prompt templates rewritten to vibesync (closed-bead IDs preserved as stable historical refs). Out-of-scope items flagged in the bead description: bd prefix rename + huly-vibe-sync.db filename rename — both require their own migration plan.
created with stale prefix during migration; superseded by config update
prefix migration verified

Landed in commit 5679348 — AGENTS.md RuntimeProvider discipline block, gastown-orchestration.md what-we-use/don't table + diagram update, gastown-role-catalog.md polecat rewrite, provider.ts header comment refresh.

Landed in 2cc0f0d. observe() polls runtime.tasks.get (no progress callback in teams SDK) and maps TaskState transitions → SessionEvent: pending→started, running→first-token, toolCalls→tool-call+tool-result, done→turn-done, error→error, stop()→stopped. 7 new vitest cases green, full orchestration suite 86/86.

Landed in dd0083b. LettaTeamsProvider accepts an optional EventBus at construction; observe() publishes one runtime/session.<kind> event per yielded SessionEvent with teammate target, active task_id, and molecule_id from spec.extra. 4 new vitest cases green; orchestration suite 90/90.

Landed in 55e4423. Teammate target resolves to extra.target ?? (moleculeId ? `${moleculeId}-${role}` : role). Two molecules with same role spawn distinct teammates; stop() on one leaves the other untouched. 4 new vitest cases green.

Landed in 7da74da. HealthPatrol gains a DaemonSupervisor seam (id, providerKind, isRunning, ensureRunning, stop). trackDaemon/restartDaemon mirror the session-restart path with same backoff + circuit-breaker config; daemon.down/restarting/restarted/restart_failed/unhealthy events go to the bus. LettaTeamsProvider.daemonSupervisor() returns an adapter that delegates to runtime.daemon.{isRunning,ensureRunning,stop}. 7 new patrol cases + 1 adapter case. Orchestration suite 102/102.

Override half landed in 09da955: LettaTeamsProvider.start passes skipInit: true on spawn so teams.init.js does NOT write to memory blocks; runTeamsInit=true opts back in. Seed-from-TOML half spun out as vibesync-6wn.9 since it lands an @letta-ai/letta-client integration on its own merits.

Landed in 0196ea8. extra.memfsStartup is now validated against the SDK's MemfsStartup union ('blocking'|'background'|'skip') and forwarded onto SpawnTeammateInput; invalid values are dropped. memfs teardown rides with teammates.remove (already called from stop) — documented in the header comment. 3 new vitest cases; orchestration suite 107/107.

Landed in 6be18c8. Pack schema parses [[memory_blocks]] off role TOMLs (label/value/optional limit). LettaTeamsProvider gains a MemoryBlockSeeder seam — orchestration layer owns the contract, never imports @letta-ai/letta-client. LettaTeamsMemoryBlockSeeder in src/letta/ is the production adapter: list existing blocks, diff by label, modify in place or create+attach; idempotent on re-seed. 16 new vitest cases across pack loader, provider, and adapter. Orchestration + letta suites 122/122.

Landed in bd2e1b4. LettaTeamsBackendConfig owns LETTA_BASE_URL/LETTA_API_KEY/LETTA_PASSWORD/LETTA_CLI_PATH reads, exposes daemonEnv() + applyToProcessEnv() for the teams daemon, and buildSeeder() returns a LettaTeamsMemoryBlockSeeder wired to a LettaClient pointed at the same backend. 9 vitest cases. Header documents the ~/.lteams/ foot-gun and links to vibesync-6wn.10.

Landed in 622cf63. TeammateDriftAuditor detects orphan_local (local entry → missing agentId) and orphan_server (server agent with no/conflicting local entry); emits one runtime/teammate.drift bus event per finding. Runbook at docs/architecture/letta-teams-state-drift.md covers symptoms / detection / cleanup / prevention rules. 7 vitest cases. CLI 'vibesync sweep teams-state' deliberately deferred — the auditor is pluggable and CLI is mechanical wiring on top.
